channel.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Weekly Updates RSS Feed
channel.description: Track daily updates of more than 500 awesome projects, including selfhosted, rust, javascript, swift, python, react, vue, free for dev, flutter, go etc.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Sep 26th - Oct 2nd, 2022
item.description:
On-line courses

  • GTFS-booksstars2 - Comprehensive guides to GTFS and GTFS Realtime. These books were written by Quentin Zervaas, and have been donated to MobilityData and made open access.

    • Other multimodal data formats

  • TIDES projectstars5 - Transit ITS Data Exchange Specification (TIDES) is a proposed effort to create standard data structures, APIs, and data management tools for historical transit ITS data including AVL, APC and AFC Data.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 27th - Jul 3rd, 2022
item.description:
GTFS Analysis Tools

  • tidytransitstars110 - An R package to read GTFS data into tibbles and simple features dataframes to map transit stops and routes, calculate travel times and transit frequencies, and validate transit feeds.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 30th - Jun 5th, 2022
item.description:
GTFS Converters

  • NeTEx-to-GTFS Converter Javastars0 - Converts NeTEX datasets into GTFS datasets. The input NeTEx datasets are required to follow the Nordic NeTEx Profile.

    • Community

  • MobilityData Slack chat

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 16th - May 22nd, 2022
item.description:
Web Apps (open source)

  • Bustime - Public transport real-time monitoring with WebSocket updates. Open-source on GitHubstars54.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 2nd - May 8th, 2022
item.description:
Python
  • transit_service_analyststars1 - A Python library to support transit service analysis.

    • GTFS Analysis Tools

  • transit_service_analyststars1 - A Python library to support transit service analysis.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 18th - Apr 24th, 2022
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Validators

  • gtfs-realtime-validatorstars13 - A GTFS Realtime validation tool originally developed by the Center for Urban Transportation Research at the University of South Florida and now maintained by MobilityData.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 11th - Apr 17th, 2022
item.description:
Proprietary (non-standard) vendor APIs

  • Navitia.io - REST API for journey planning, stop schedules, isocrhons and lot more on US and EU. Navitiastars380 is the opensource engine behind the live API.

    • Software for Creating APIs

  • Navitiastars380 is the opensource engine behind the Navitia.io live API.

    • GTFS Converters

  • transit_modelstars37 - A Rust library to convert to/from the following formats: GTFS, NTFS (for Navitia, see Software for Creating APIs), TransXChange (UK standard format), KV1 (Netherland standard format) or NeTEx (European standard format).

    • Other multimodal data formats

  • Cal-ITP Operational Data Standard (ODS) - Intended to address the lack of a standard format for representing transit schedules used by drivers, dispatchers, and planners to carry out transit operations.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 4th - Apr 10th, 2022
item.description:
3rd party GTFS URL directories

  • OpenMobilityData (Deprecated) - List of GTFS and GTFS-RT feeds. Archives and validates the GTFS feeds and allows you to preview both GTFS and GTFS-RT through the browser. Formerly TransitFeeds.com. MobilityData announced it is end-of-life as of early 2022 with a shutdown date to be determined.
  • The Mobility Database - JSON and CSV files on GitHubstars78 that is a repository of 1300+ mobility datasets across the world. Contains contents of OpenMobilityData/TransitFeeds.com.
  • Transitland - Community editable list of many transit agency GTFS datasets. Also provides an API to access the data as JSON/GeoJSON and a playground to try out the data.

    • Web Apps (open source)

  • Tiramisu Transitstars3 - An adaptive mobile transit app that shows real-time bus arrival information developed and deployed by Carnegie Mellon University. No longer maintained.

    • Native Apps (open source)

  • Tiramisu Transitstars3 - An adaptive mobile transit app that shows real-time bus arrival information developed and deployed by Carnegie Mellon University. Written using Ionic framework. No longer maintained.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 21st - Mar 27th, 2022
item.description:
Sample GTFS and GTFS Realtime datasets used for software testing

  • transitfeed unit tests - Test data created for the original Google Python GTFS validator.
  • Transitland GTFS and GTFS Realtime unit testsstars16 - For testing the transitland-libstars16 library that handles GTFS and GTFS Realtime parsing and validation for Transitland:
    • GTFS - "bad entities" at the single row level
    • GTFS - validation errors that that involve entities in one or more files
    • GTFS - best practices
    • GTFS Realtime - JSON test cases that are turned into PB contents
  • gtfs-realtime-validator unit tests - Some GTFS datasets (zip files) are included and a large number of GTFS RT messages are defined programmatically in Java via the gtfs-realtime-bindings library.
  • OpenTripPlanner unit tests - Some GTFS datasets are defined for the unit tests (GtfsTest and mmri folder).

    • GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • GTFS Editorstars131 - A (self-hosted) web-based GTFS editing framework. (Note: this project has been deprecated in favor of IBI Data Toolsstars88.)
  • GTFS Editor for Vagrantstars5 - Quickly set up the GTFS editor (above) using Vagrant
  • gtfs-blocks-to-transfersstars4 - A Python tool to convert GTFS blocks, defined by setting trip.block_id into a series of trip-to-trip transfers (proposal).

    • Visualizations

  • Transit Visstars3 - A visualization tool to display performance metrics derived from the King County Metro GTFS-RT feed (OneBusAway API). Viewable here. Used for this paper.

    • Academic papers

  • Aemmer et al. - "Measurement and classification of transit delays using GTFS-RT data" - Presents a method for extracting transit performance metrics from a General Transit Feed Specification’s Real-Time (GTFS-RT) component and aggregating them to roadway segments. Used with Transit Visstars3, viewable here.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 14th - Mar 20th, 2022
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Libraries & Demo Apps

  • GTFS-realtime Vehicle Positions Consumer/Visualizer Demostars44 - A Java-based demo project for consuming a GTFS-realtime Vehicle Positions feed and displaying this info on a map.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 28th - Mar 6th, 2022
item.description:
GTFS Converters

  • hafas-generate-gtfsstars4 (work-in-progress) – A Javascript tool to generate GTFS dumps from HAFAS endpoints.
  • GTFS-route-shapesstars12 - A Python script to generate a single geoJSON shape for each transit route in a GTFS archive.

    • Sample GTFS and GTFS Realtime datasets used for software testing

  • sample-gtfs-feedstars7 - An imaginary GTFS data set used for testing.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 21st - Feb 27th, 2022
item.description:
Visualizations

  • gtfs-visualizationsstars108 - Open-source NodeJS application for visualizing the routes of GTFS datasets.
  • fastest-bus-analysis-in-the-weststars14 - A python Pandas script that combines Ridership/APC, Swiftly speed and dwell data, bus stop inventory, GTFS, and geospatial shapes to create a stop by stop, route by route, time grouping filterable dataset for cross-analyses. The dataset is then visualized in Tableau to help VTA Planners find places to make bus and rail network faster and more reliable through speedups methods like stop consolidation and dedicated lanes.
  • MIT COAXS - Co-creative Planning of Transit Corridors using Accessibility-Based Stakeholder Engagement (shows route scenarios using OpenTripPlanner Analyst).
  • MOTIS - Intermodal Mobility Information System including visualization
  • MTA Frequency - Frequency visualization of subways and buses in New York City built using Transitland.
  • SEPTA Rail OTP Report - An online on-time performance reporing & drill down tool using GTFS.
  • Toronto Transit Explorerstars35 - A Java application that visualizes transit, biking and walking accessibility across the city of Toronto. Live version hosted here. Uses a modified version of R5stars202 for routing.
  • TransitFlowstars261 Animate GTFS data around the world using Processing and Transitland.
  • TRAVIC Transit Visualization Client - Visualizes vehicles moving based on static GTFS data (and sometimes realtime data). Supports over 260 cities. Github account for geOps organization is here.
  • Traze by Veridict - Visualization of public transport vehicles from all over the world. Collaborate with other users to get real-time updates even when it is not available from the agency. Based on a number of sources, including GTFS and GTFS-RT. (Previously known as Livemap24).
  • Visualizing MBTA Data - Interactive graphs that show how people use Boston's subway system.

    • GTFS Realtime Utilities

  • Concentratestars19 - Combines realtime transit information from multiple sources into single output files. Maintained by [

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 14th - Feb 20th, 2022
item.description:
GTFS Analysis Tools

  • R5: Rapid Realistic Routing on Real-world and Reimagined networksstars202 - A Java-based routing engine developed by Conveyal for multimodal (transit/bike/walk/car) networks. It currently plans many trips over a time window for scenario planning and analytics purposes. A related R wrapper package (r5rstars112) is developed independently by IPEA. See also the performance comparison from Higgins et al. (2022), linked below.

    • Academic papers

  • Higgins et al. - "Calculating place-based transit accessibility: Methods, tools and algorithmic dependence" (2022) - Compares software tools for calculating accessibility by walking and public transit including ArcGIS Pro, Emme, R5R, and OpenTripPlanner.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 10th - Jan 16th, 2022
item.description:
Other multimodal data formats

  • Dyno-Demandstars2 - A GTFS-based travel demand data format focusing on individual passenger demand suitable for dynamic network modeling developed by San Francisco County Transportation Authority, LMZ LLC, and UrbanLabs LLC.

    • Software for Creating APIs

  • GraphHopper Routing Enginestars3.8k Open source routing engine for OpenStreetMap. Use it as Java library or server.
  • gtfs-serverstars18 - A web server, written in Rust that uses PostGIS as a backend to serve GTFS data via a HTTP endpoint
  • hafas-rest-apistars11 – Expose a HAFAS endpoint as a REST API.
  • Linked Connections - An open-source, scalable intermodal route planning engine, which allows clients to execute the route planning algorithm (as opposed to the server). Uses GTFS data.
  • pyBikesstars492 - Software powering CityBikes for worldwide bikeshare system info
  • MOTISstars70 - Multi Objective Travel Information System, written in C++ and Java. Can consume schedule timetables in the GTFS or HAFAS format as well as real time information in the GTFS-RT (and RISML, a propriatary format at Deutsche Bahn) as input data. For pedestrian routing (handled by Per Pedes Routing) and car routing (handled by OSRM) OpenStreetMap data is used.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 3rd - Jan 9th, 2022
item.description:
Proprietary (non-standard) vendor APIs

  • Citymapper API - REST API for transit journey planning, realtime transit data and walk, cycle, scooter travel times.

    • SDKs

  • Citymapper SDK - Allows you to add journey planning and turn-by-turn navigation for walking, cycling, scooting and transit into native iOS and Android apps.
    • Python
  • combine_gtfs_feedsstars4 - A Python tool to combine multiple gtfs feeds into one feed/dataset.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Dec 6th - Dec 12th, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Utilities

  • bus_kalmanstars19 - A Kalman Filter used to interpolate bus travel times using NYC MTA real-time data.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Nov 8th - Nov 14th, 2021
item.description:
Academic papers

  • Gramacki et al. - "gtfs2vec - Learning GTFS Embeddings for comparing Public Transport Offer in Microregions" - Methology using Uber's H3 spatial index and machine learning to identify areas of "similar" public transit service quality in cities. Source code available on GitHubstars5.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Nov 1st - Nov 7th, 2021
item.description:
Community

  • German Open Transport Meetup - You can participate in a biweekly meetup of the German-speaking open transport community.
  • OpenTripPlannerstars1.8k Community
    • OpenTripPlanner User mailing list
    • OpenTripPlanner Developers mailing list

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Oct 25th - Oct 31st, 2021
item.description:
Native Apps (open source)

  • Trufi Appstars1 - A cross-platform Flutter app that uses OpenTripPlanner
  • MACS Transit Android Appstars8 - A bus tracker app for Android devices for the MACS Transit system in Fairbanks, Alaska. Uses RouteMatch APIs.
  • OneBusAway Apps - Android (source code), Fire Phone (source code), iOS (source code), Windows Phone (source code), Windows 8 (source code), Google Glass GDK (source code), Alexa skill (source code)

    • GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • GTFS Text-to-Speech Testerstars1 - A command-line tool that reads GTFS stop names out loud using Text-to-Speech to determine which need Text-to-Speech values for tts_stop_name in stops.txt.

    • GTFS Timetable Publishing Tools

  • GTFS-to-HTML - Generate human-readable timetables in HTML or PDF format directly from GTFS.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Oct 4th - Oct 10th, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Analysis Tools

  • GTFS-to-Chartstars16 - Creates stringline charts showing all vehicles on a transit route from GTFS data.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Sep 6th - Sep 12th, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • SIRI to GTFS-realtime - A Java-based command-line utility to convert from the SIRI format to GTFS-realtime

    • SIRI

  • SIRI API - Java classes generated from the v1.0 and v1.3 SIRI schemas.
  • SIRI 2.0 APIstars6 - Java classes generated from the v2.0 SIRI schemas.
  • SIRI to GTFS-realtime - A Java-based command-line utility to convert from the SIRI format to GTFS-realtime.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 23rd - Aug 29th, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Converters

  • gtfs2gpsstars60 - An R package that converts public transportation data in GTFS format to GPS-like records in a data.table, where each row represents the timestamp of each vehicle at a given spatial resolution.

    • GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • TransitWandstars36 - An open source web and mobile application for collecting transit data. Use it to create GTFS feeds, capture passenger counts or generate GIS datasets.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 9th - Aug 15th, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Validators

  • gtfstidystars76 - A Go-based tool to tidy and validate GTFS feeds.
  • Transit App's gtfs-fares-v2-validatorstars6 - A Python tool that validators GTFS-Fares-v2 data based on the draft specification.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 14th - Jun 20th, 2021
item.description:
Crowdsourced transit data

  • GTFS-Hubstars19 - Community tested, probably quality/content enhanced, partially merged or filtered GTFS-feeds of (currently German) transport agencies. Maintained by MITFAHR|DE|ZENTRALE.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 7th - Jun 13th, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Validators

  • Conveyal's gtfs-validatorstars37 - A Java-based GTFS validator based on the OneBusAway GTFS Modules, runs in Java and is faster than the Google provided one.
  • Conveyal's gtfs-libstars52 - Conveyal's successor to their own gtfs-validatorstars37, a Java-based library for loading and saving GTFS feeds of arbitrary size with disk-backed storage.
  • Google's feedValidator - Google-supported Python-based GTFS validator.
  • GTFS Meta-Validator (hosted by Omni) - A web-based GTFS validator that runs both the Google Python feedValidator and Conveyal's gtfs-validatorstars37 on uploaded GTFS files.
  • gtfs-validator-apistars1 - This Python package is a thin wrapper around MobilityData/gtfs-validatorstars130 that handles intermediate files produced and finds gtfs-validator's output file so it can be given a specific name or returned as a string.
  • MobilityData's gtfs-validatorstars130 - A open-source GTFS validator canonically following the GTFS spec implemented in Java licensed under Apache v2.0 maintained by MobilityData.
  • Reflect GTFS Validator (hosted by Foursquare ITP) - Transit schedule and GTFS validation platform by Foursquare ITP that includes a free, web-based GTFS validator based on gtfs-libstars52.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 31st - Jun 6th, 2021
item.description:
Native Apps (open source)

  • opentripplanner-client-librarystars1 - A Kotlin Multiplatform library for making API requests and parsing responses from an OpenTripPlanner v2 server for trip plans, bike rental info, and server metadata for Android, iOS, and web.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 17th - May 23rd, 2021
item.description:
Native Apps (closed source)

  • Ualabee - Community driven trip planner with focus on user interaction, users can report anomalies, upload pictures, edit transit data and chat with other passengers.

    • GTFS Converters

  • kml-to-gtfs-shapes - Javascript tool to convert polylines from a KML file into a GTFS shapes.txt file. Hosted on GitHub here.
  • extract-gtfs-pathwaysstars1 – Command-line tool to extract pathways as GeoJSON from a GTFS dataset.
  • extract-gtfs-shapes – Command-line tool to extract shapes as GeoJSON from a GTFS dataset.
  • gtfs-service-areastars2 - Compute a transit service area from static GTFS. Results are output as single-layer .geojson files. Dockerized version of gtfs-to-geojsonstars70.
  • gtfs-to-geojsonstars70 - Javascript tool that converts transit data in GTFS shapes and stops into geoJSON. This is useful for creating maps of transit routes.
  • Hafas2GTFSstars14 - Hafas2GTFS converter written in Python, optimized for SBB HAFAS feeds.
  • o2gstars9 - A simple tool to extract GTFS feed from OpenStreetMap.
  • Open-Transport SYNTHESE Convertors - Converts French-Transmodel, SIRI, NETeX, HAFAS, HASTUS, VDV452, and more.
  • onebusaway-gtfs-to-barefootstars1 - A Java tool to create a Barefootstars609 mapfile from a GTFS file.
  • osm2gtfsstars81 - Turn OpenStreetMap data and schedule information into GTFS.
  • transloc-gtfs-rectifierstars2 - Python application that attempts to assign GTFS stop_ids to TransLoc IDs using TransLoc's API (TransLoc doesn't provide GTFS stop_ids in their API).
  • Transmodel and IFF to GTFSstars4 - Imports and syncs (Transmodel) BISON Koppelvlak1, IFF (a format written by HP/EDS, somewhat similiar to ATCO CIF) to import timetables of the railway networks. The internal pseudo-NETeX datastructure allows to export to GTFS and there are proof-of-concepts to export to other formats such as NETeX, GTFS and IFF.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 12th - Apr 18th, 2021
item.description:
Academic papers

  • Brakewood and Watkins - "A literature review of the passenger benefits of real-time transit information" (2018) - An overview of many different research studies looking at the benefits of real-time transit information.

    • Government reports

  • TCRP Synthesis 115 - Open Data: Challenges and Opportunities for Transit Agencies (2015) - A comprehensive report looking at the benefits and challenges of open transit data.
  • TCRP Research Report 213: Data Sharing Guidance for Public Transit Agencies – Now and in the Future (2020) - A report designed to help agencies make decisions about sharing their data, including how to evaluate benefits, costs, and risks.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 5th - Apr 11th, 2021
item.description:
Other multimodal data formats

  • Open Sales and Distribution Model (OSDM)stars23 - Aims to substantially simplify the booking process for customers of rail trips and to lower complexity and distribution costs for distributors and railway carriers. Contains a specification of an offline model and on-line API. Maintained by the International Union of Railways (UIC).
  • Alliance for Parking Data Standards (APDS) - Formed by the International Parking Institute (IPI), the British Parking Association (BPA), and the European Parking Association (EPA), APDS is a not-for-profit organization with the mission to develop, promote, manage, and maintain a uniform global standard that will allow organizations to share parking data across platforms worldwide. APDS Version 1.0 documents are here.
  • CurbLRstars69 - A specification for curb regulations.
  • Dyno-Pathstars1 - (Under development - see this post) Data for individual passenger trajectories.
  • GTFS-flexstars90 - A data format that models flexible public transportation services as an extension to GTFS.
  • GTFS-ridestars43 - An open, fixed-route transit ridership data standard developed through a partnership between the Oregon Department of Transportation and Oregon State University.
  • General Modeling Network Specification (GMNS)stars73 - A format for sharing routable road network files designed to be used in multi-modal static and dynamic transportation planning and operations models. Volpe/FHWA partnership with Zephyr Foundation.
  • General Travel Network Specification - A planned data specification for sharing travel demand model networks.
  • Managed and Tolled Lanes Feed Specification (MTLFS)stars9 - Proposal for a schema that comprise the Managed and Tolled Lanes Tolling Feed Specification (MTLFS) and defines the fields used in all of those files developed by Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority.
  • Mobility Data Specification (MDS)stars604 - A format to implement realtime data sharing, measurement and regulation for municipalities and mobility as a service providers. It is meant to ensure that governments have the ability to enforce, evaluate and manage providers. Maintained by the Open Mobility Foundation.
  • NeTex - A general purpose XML format designed for the exchange of complex static transport data among distributed systems managed by the CEN standards process.
  • SAE Shared and Digital Mobility Committee - Appears to be working on a data standard for car share and transportation network companies (TNCs) / rideshare.
  • TCRP G-16 Development of Transactional Data Specifications for Demand-Responsive Transportation (In progress) - The objective of this research is to develop technical specifications for transactional data for entities involved in the provision of demand-responsive transportation. Expected completion date is late 2018.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 29th - Apr 4th, 2021
item.description:
Web Apps (open source)

  • 1-Click - A virtual “trip aggregator” that assembles information on a wide variety of available modes: public transit, private, rail, rideshare, carpool, volunteer, paratransit, and walking and biking. Open-source on GitHubstars13.
  • MBTA tile-serverstars6 - Scripts to create a Docker container that encapsulates all the elements necessary to develop map tiles for use on MBTA.com

    • Web Apps (closed source)

  • TransitScreen - Custom realtime displays of all local transportation choices
  • Citylines.co - A collaborative platform for mapping transit systems, with an emphasis on their historical evolution.
  • Bikeshare Map - Status of all worldwide bikeshare stations
  • Bongo - Real-time Transit Tracking for Iowa City, Coralville and the University of Iowa. Combines three disparate transit systems into one UI.
  • Brand New Subway - An interactive transportation planning game that lets players alter the NYC subway system to their heart's content.
  • CityMapper Webapp - Really polished webapp with trip planner and route status for over 30 of cities.
  • YourStop - Mobile friendly web app which consumes GTFS feeds and displays both live and scheduled trips for stops. Launched with MBTA, YRT/Viva and Maryland MTA.
  • DC MetroHero - Realtime vehicle position and arrivals and departure information for the Washington, D.C. region's WMATA Metrorail and Metrobus systems. WebApp, Android, and iOS apps avaliable.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 22nd - Mar 28th, 2021
item.description:
Web Apps (open source)

  • Dede - An independent and universal passenger information system (PIS) mapping realtime movement. A message feed with Vehicle Position entities in the GTFS-Realtime format or the Dede appstars1 can be used as data source.

    • Native Apps (open source)

  • Dede Appstars1 - An app making any Android powered phone become an Automatic Vehicle Locating (AVL) device for the Dede passenger information system (PIS).
    • PostgreSQL
  • gtfs-via-postgresstars33 – Yet another tool to process GTFS using PostgreSQL.

    • Other multimodal data formats

  • Transport Operator Mobility-as-a-service Provider (TOMP)-APIstars73 - Working group in the Netherlands with a goal to develop an API for use by Transport Operators and Mobility-as-a-service Providers for operator discovery, trip planning, end user interaction, booking, and payment.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 15th - Mar 21st, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Analysis Tools

  • ESRI ArcGIS Public Transit Tools (GTFS)stars127 - Tools for working with public transit data in ArcGIS

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 1st - Mar 7th, 2021
item.description:
Other multimodal data formats

  • shared-rowstars16 - A specification for right-of-way (ROW) for a SharedStreets Reference.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 15th - Feb 21st, 2021
item.description:
On-line courses

  • Planetizen "Building a Transit Map Web App" course - A video tutorial on setting up your own web-based mapping application, with no coding experience required.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 4th - Jan 10th, 2021
item.description:
GTFS Validators

  • Transport Validatorstars25 - An open-source validator implemented in Rust. Used by the French National Access Point.

    • GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • GTFS-realtime to SIRI-Litestars18 - A Rust webserver to convert multiple GTFS-RT feeds to a SIRI-Lite API.

    • GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • gtfs-station-builderstars11 - UI tool to help build the internal structure of stations (including pathways.txt)

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Dec 7th - Dec 13th, 2020
item.description:
Blog posts

  • "How to use GTFS data to track transit vehicles in realtime" by Tom Camp - Using GTFS and GTFS Realtime to provide continuous realtime updates.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Nov 30th - Dec 6th, 2020
item.description:
JavaScript
  • Node-GTFSstars364 - Loads transit data from GTFS files, unzips it and stores it to a SQLite database. Provides some methods to query for agencies, routes, stops and times.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Nov 23rd - Nov 29th, 2020
item.description:
Getting started

  • MBTA GTFS Onboarding - An interactive tutorial created by MBTA for GTFS static. A stand-alone Docker imagestars6 is available on GitHub as well as a hosted/no-install version of the Jupyter notebook.

    • On-line courses

  • MBTA GTFS Onboarding - An interactive tutorial created by MBTA for GTFS static. A stand-alone Docker imagestars6 is available on GitHub as well as a hosted/no-install version of the Jupyter notebook.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 3rd - Aug 9th, 2020
item.description:
Crowdsourced transit data

  • Citylines.co - A collaborative platform for mapping transit systems, with an emphasis on their historical evolution. The data can be downloaded as GeoJSON or CSV from citylines.co/data.
  • OpenStreetMap (OSM) - The collaborative platform for mapping the world, including transport, transit, and routing data.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jul 6th - Jul 12th, 2020
item.description:
C++
  • just_gtfsstars13 - C++17 header-only library for reading and writing GTFS (used in MAPS.MEstars4.5k). Main features: fast reading and writing of GTFS feeds, support for extended GTFS route types, simple working with GTFS Date and Time formats.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 15th - Jun 21st, 2020
item.description:
Other multimodal data formats

  • OMX: The Open Matrix data file formatstars40 - A structured collection of two-dimensional array objects and associated metadata, for possible use in the transportation modeling industry.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 8th - Jun 14th, 2020
item.description:
GTFS Analysis Tools

  • Busbuzzardstars7 - Inference of probabilistic schedules from empirical data about transit vehicles.
    • Python
  • GTFS Kitstars0 - A Python 3.6+ tool kit for analyzing General Transit Feed Specification (GTFS) data. Supersedes GTFSTK.
  • GTFSTK - A Python 3 toolkit for analyzing GTFS data in memory. Uses Pandas and Shapely for speed. Superseded by GTFS Kitstars0.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 1st - Jun 7th, 2020
item.description:
National government datasets

  • European long-distance transport operators (EU) (Unofficial)stars46 - Unofficial list of available API endpoints, GTFS feeds and client libraries

    • Proprietary (non-standard) vendor APIs

  • HAFAS – Propriety public transport management software by HaCon (list of endpoints)
    • JavaScript
  • gtfs-utilsstars22 – Utilities to process GTFS data sets (e.g., "flattening" calendar.txt & calendar_dates.txt, computing arrival/departure times of trips).
  • gtfs-via-postgresstars33 – Yet another tool to process GTFS using PostgreSQL.

    • GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • hafas-gtfs-rt-feedstars5 – A Javascript tool to generate a GTFS Realtime feed from a HAFAS endpoint.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 25th - May 31st, 2020
item.description:
GTFS Validators

  • GTFSVTORstars24 - An open-source GTFS validator implemented in Java licensed under GPLv3 maintained by Mecatran.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 18th - May 24th, 2020
item.description:
Web Apps (open source)

  • GTFS Builder - A free web-based application to help you create GTFS files. Maintained by the National Rural Transit Assistance Program (RTAP).

    • GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • GTFS Builder - A free web-based application to help you create GTFS files. Maintained by the National Rural Transit Assistance Program (RTAP).
    • C
  • CGTFSstars4 - C library for reading static GTFS feeds. Supports reading unpacked feeds into application memory or into SQLite databases.
  • RRRR Rapid Real-time Routingstars147 - RRRR (usually pronounced R4) is a C-language implementation of the RAPTOR public transit routing algorithm.
    • C#
  • ESRI public-transit-toolsstars127 - Tools for working with public transit data in ArcGIS (license for ArcGIS required).
  • GTFS Feed Parserstars55 - .Net/Mono implementation of a GTFS parser.
    • Go
  • Go GTFS Parserstars39 - A GTFS parsing library for Go.
    • PostgreSQL
  • gtfs-schemastars25 - PostgreSQL schema for GTFS feeds.
    • Python
  • ESRI public-transit-toolsstars127 - Tools for working with public transit data in ArcGIS (license for ArcGIS required).
  • gtfsdbstars124 - Python library for converting GTFS files into a relational database.
  • gtfsmanstars20 - Repository-like tool in Python to manage and update a huge number of GTFS feeds.
  • gtfspystars125 - Public transport network analysis and travel time computations using Python3. Compatible with Postgres/PostGIS, Oracle, MySQL, and SQLite. Used by gtfspy-webvizstars33.
  • Make GTFS - A Python library to make GTFS feeds from basic route information.
  • Mapzen GTFSstars27 - A Python GTFS library that supports reading individual GTFS tables, or constructing a graph to represent each agency in a feed.
  • multigtfsstars49 - A Django application to import and export GTFS.
    • Ruby
  • GTFS-vizstars80 - Ruby script that converts a set of GTFS files into a SQLite database + GeoJSONs (needed by the Transit Mapstars325 web application)

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 11th - May 17th, 2020
item.description:
GTFS Realtime (and Other Real-time API) Archival Tools

  • GTFS-realtime to SQL - Parses a GTFS-RealTime feed into an SQL database (used in OpenMobilityData.org)

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 4th - May 10th, 2020
item.description:
GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • GTFS shape mapfitstars7 - Python tool that fits GTFS shape files and stops to a given OSM map file. Uses pymapmatchstars3 for the matching.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 13th - Apr 19th, 2020
item.description:
Web Apps (open source)

  • Transit Tracker - Realtime vehicle position for Greater Montreal & Toronto, Canada

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 9th - Mar 15th, 2020
item.description:
GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • IBI Data Toolsstars88 - A web application that handles GTFS editing, validating, quality checking, and deploying to OpenTripPlanner. (Combines and builds upon the functionality of the deprecated Gtfs Data Managerstars26 and GTFS Editorstars131.)

    • National government datasets

  • National Transit Database (USA) - Information and statistics on the transit systems of the United States, run by the Federal Transit Administration.
  • Transport (France) - GTFS datasets for French transit systems.

    • Visualizations

  • All Transit - Interactive GTFS route and schedule animation (for U.S. cities) using Mapbox GL JS, Deck.gl and Transitland. Github repository herestars20.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 2nd - Mar 8th, 2020
item.description:
Blog posts

  • "Visualizing Better Transportation: Data & Tools" by Steve Pepple - A collection of transportation-related data and tools for the San Francisco Bay Area and other cities in North America, originally collected and discussed at a 2018 Transit Week Event at ARUP in San Francisco.

    • Web Apps (open source)

  • OTP-UI React Component Librarystars34 - React Javascript component library, which can be used to build trip planner webapps. See the Storybook for a demo.
  • OpenTripPlanner.jsstars19 - A Javascript-based client for OpenTripPlanner (no longer under development)

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 20th - Jan 26th, 2020
item.description:
GTFS Converters

  • onebusaway-vdv-modulesstars11 - A Java library for working with transit data in the VDV format, including converting VDV-452 schedule data into GTFS.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Dec 2nd - Dec 8th, 2019
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • Transloc API to GTFS-realtimestars2 - A Node.js based tool to convert the Transloc API to GTFS-realtime.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Nov 18th - Nov 24th, 2019
item.description:
GTFS Converters

  • gtsfstars3 - general transit (GTFS) simple (geographic) features (sf) in R. can be used to convert from GTFS to Shapefile, GeoJSON, and other formats through GDAL.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Oct 7th - Oct 13th, 2019
item.description:
Agency Tools

  • TBEST - TBEST (Transit Boardings Estimation and Simulation Tool) is an effort to develop a multi-faceted GIS-based modeling, planning and analysis tool which integrates socio-economic, land use, and transit network data into a platform for scenario-based transit ridership estimation and analysis. Funded by the Florida Department of Transportation. Free to use but not open-source.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jul 15th - Jul 21st, 2019
item.description:
SIRI

  • Edwigstars16 - A golang server for real-time public transport data exchange, using the SIRI protocol.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jul 8th - Jul 14th, 2019
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • gtfs-realtime-translatorsstars19 - A Python-based tool to translate custom arrival API formats to GTFS-realtime. As of July 2019 it supports LA Metro and SEPTA.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 3rd - Jun 9th, 2019
item.description:
Agency Tools

  • Route Trends (webapp, GitHubstars11) - An R Shiny app to ingest ridership time series, and return seasonal, trend, and residual components according to STL methodology and forecasts including uncertainty based on those components. Sponsored by Metro Transit (Minneapolis-St. Paul).

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 27th - Jun 2nd, 2019
item.description:
GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • static-GTFS-managerstars122 - A (self-hosted) browser-based user interface for creating, editing, exporting static GTFS (see related post). Live demo here.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 18th - Mar 24th, 2019
item.description:
Community

  • Transit Techies NYC - NYC-based meetup for those interested in this repo. Speaker list includes many contributors to this repo.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 4th - Feb 10th, 2019
item.description:
GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • pfaedlestars122 - Precise map-matching for GTFS using OpenStreetMap data

    • Other multimodal data formats

  • NCHRP 08-119 Developing Data Standards and Guidance for Transportation Planning and Traffic Operations - Phase 1 (Anticipated) - The objective of this research is to develop standards and/or guidance to be used and adopted by the transportation community in collecting, managing, and sharing static and real-time data for transportation planning and operations.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 14th - Jan 20th, 2019
item.description:
Native Apps (open source)

  • Offi Directions - An Android app that provides trip planning, schedules, live departure times, and disruption information for transport authorities in Europe and beyond.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 7th - Jan 13th, 2019
item.description:
Getting started

  • Open Transit Data Toolkit - A series of lessons to help people utilize open transit data.

    • On-line courses

  • Open Transit Data Toolkit - A series of lessons to help people utilize open transit data.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Nov 12th - Nov 18th, 2018
item.description:
Agency Tools

  • TNExTstars12 - Transit Network Explorer Tool (TNExT) is a web-based software tool developed for the visualization, analysis, and reporting of regional and statewide transit networks in the state of Oregon.

    • Visualizations

  • TNExTstars12 - Transit Network Explorer Tool (TNExT) is a web-based software tool developed for the visualization, analysis, and reporting of regional and statewide transit networks in the state of Oregon.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Oct 15th - Oct 21st, 2018
item.description:
GTFS Analysis Tools

  • Peartreestars144 - A Python library for converting transit data into a directed graph for network analysis.
  • transitrstars18 - An R package for constructing and modelling a transit network in real time to obtain vehicle ETAs

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Oct 1st - Oct 7th, 2018
item.description:
GTFS Timetable Publishing Tools

  • TimeTablePublisher (TTPUB)stars24 - A web publishing system developed by TriMet that allows a transit agency to examine, modify, and transform raw scheduling data into easy-to-read timetables for customer information purposes

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Sep 3rd - Sep 9th, 2018
item.description:
GTFS Realtime

  • GTFS-realtime Autodoc - Automatically generated documentation for GTFS-realtime, generated from the official GTFS-realtime protocol buffer specification and including some extensions.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 27th - Sep 2nd, 2018
item.description:
Government reports

  • TCRP G-16 Development of Transactional Data Specifications for Demand-Responsive Transportation (In progress) - The objective of this research is to develop technical specifications for transactional data for entities involved in the provision of demand-responsive transportation. Expected completion date is late 2018.

    • Other multimodal data formats

  • Mobility as a Service API - A set of open documents and test suite that defines a MaaS-compatible API (e.g., a MaaS Transport Service Provider Booking API).

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jul 2nd - Jul 8th, 2018
item.description:
R
  • trreadstars8 - A transit (GTFS) file reader for R.

    • SIRI

  • SIRI 2.0 Autodoc - Automatically generated documentation from the (incredibly well) annotated SIRI 2.0 Schema Definition.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 18th - Jun 24th, 2018
item.description:
Visualizations

  • gtfspy-webvizstars33 - Web application for animation and visualization of GTFS data using gtfspystars125.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 11th - Jun 17th, 2018
item.description:
GTFS

  • GTFS Spec - Specification for the General Transit Data Feed, or GTFS. Also available in Español, Français.

    • GTFS Realtime

  • GTFS-realtime documentation. Also available in Español.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 30th - May 6th, 2018
item.description:
Agency Tools

  • RidePilotstars15 - An open-source Computer Aided Scheduling and Dispatch (CASD) software system to meet the needs of small scale human service transportation agencies (for more info see Cambridge Systematics's marketing site).

    • GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • OrbCAD SQL Server to GTFS-realtimestars16 - A Java-based command-line utility that extracts vehicle positions and trip updates information from an OrbCAD SQL Server and exports them to the GTFS-realtime TripUpdates and VehiclePositions formats.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 23rd - Apr 29th, 2018
item.description:
Visualizations

  • Mapnificent - Shows areas you can reach with public transport in a given time. Open-source on GitHubstars334, live at https://www.mapnificent.net/.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 16th - Apr 22nd, 2018
item.description:
Agency Tools

  • TransAM - An open-source asset management platform for public transportation agencies. Open-source on Githubstars13.
  • AC Transit RestroomFinderstars0 - Pinpoints the nearest authorized restroom for bus operator and field staff, using GPS and on-screen map.
  • AC Transit Training and Education Department (TED) applicationstars7 - This application supports the District's training operations for transportation and maintenance employees, primarily in the positions of Bus Operators and Heavy Duty Coach Mechanics (Apprentice and Journey), although the system supports new courses and apprenticeship programs.
  • AC Transit Customer Relations application (CusRel)stars6 - Public transit ticketing system for customer issues and feedback with: inter-departmental routing with notifications, department/person assigments, simple workflow, ticket searching, pre-canned reports, daily reminders and more.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 9th - Apr 15th, 2018
item.description:
Community-maintained lists

  • Vendors Providing GTFS Creation/Maintenance services - Add new vendors here.
  • Entities Providing Transportation Software Development Consulting Services - Add new entities here.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 2nd - Apr 8th, 2018
item.description:
Blog posts

  • "GTFS Best Practices now available!" by Sean Barbeau - Discusses some of the challenges of an open data format like GTFS and the GTFS Best Practices that were launched in early 2017 to help address data quality.
  • When(ish) is my bus? Data and codestars43 - The data and code (R) behind Whenish is my bus? Data includes three days of historical vehicle positions and the survey results.
  • "Legacy AVL system? It's okay, join the club." by Kurt Raschke - Discussion of options for transforming legacy AVL system data into the GTFS-realtime format.
  • "What's new in GTFS-realtime v2.0" by Sean Barbeau - Discuss the shortfalls in GTFS-realtime v1.0 and the improvements in v2.0.
  • "AVL, CAD, and Real-Time Passenger Info for Beginners" by Tony Laidig - Provides a general introduction to technology used to track vehicles.

    • Academic papers

  • Tang et al. - "Ridership effects of real-time bus information system: A case study in the City of Chicago" - Experiment in Chicago, IL showed modest increase in ridership when riders had access to real-time info via text message or email.
  • Kay et al. - "When(ish) is my bus? User-centered Visualizations of Uncertainty in Everyday, Mobile Predictive Systems" - Paper attempts to answr the question of "how do we communicate uncertainty in transit predictions?" Explains the problem, existing solutions and designs a better interface for letting users know when to arrive at the bus stop.
  • Watkins et al. - "Where Is My Bus? Impact of mobile real-time information on the perceived and actual wait time of transit riders" - Experiments in Seattl,e WA showed that riders perceived shorter bus wait times when they had access to real-time info via mobile apps.
  • Brakewood et al. - “An experiment evaluating the impacts of real-time transit information on bus riders in Tampa, Florida” - Controlled experiment in Tampa, FL showed that riders with access to real-time info via mobile apps perceived nearly 2 minute reduction in wait times compared to riders without real-time info. Riders with real-time info also had decreases in anxiety and frustration and better reception of agency.
  • Brakewood et al. - "The impact of real-time information on bus ridership in New York City" - Experiment in NYC showed that ridership increased on long routes when real-time info was made available to riders.

    • 3rd party GTFS URL directories

  • GTFS Data Exchange (Deprecated) - Formerly the definitive directory of GTFS feed URLs. Shutdown in 2016. But 93 GB of data from 2008 to 2016 is available upon request.

    • Transit agency data archives

  • CapMetricsstars15 - Historical vehicle locations for Austin's transit agency (CapMetro). Data is collected by capmetricsdstars10, a Go daemon.

    • Proprietary (non-standard) vendor APIs

  • Transport API - REST API for aggregated transit data for the United Kingdom. Fee-based access.
  • TransLoc OpenAPI - REST API for real-time vehicle, route, stop, and arrival data for over 60 transit systems in the United States that have purchased TransLoc's AVL hardware and software.
  • NextBus API - REST API for real-time vehicle, route, stop, and arrival data for agencies that have puchased NextBus's hardware and/or software.
  • CityBikes - REST API for aggregated bikeshare data from around the world. Powered by pyBikesstars492.

    • Software for Creating APIs

  • TransitClock - Java application that can consume raw vehicle positions and generate prediction times in formats such as GTFS-realtime. Formerly known as "Transitime".

    • On-line courses

  • World Bank - "Intro. to GTFS" online course - A free, online, self-paced course for learning about GTFS and GTFS-realtime.

    • Government reports

  • APTA Policy Development and Research - Public Transportation Embracing Open Data - APTA's discussion of the benefits and challenges of open transit data (a short summary of the below TCRP report).

    • Getting started

  • World Bank - "Intro. to GTFS" online course - A free, online, self-paced course for learning about GTFS and GTFS-realtime.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 26th - Apr 1st, 2018
item.description:
SIRI

  • pysiri2validatorstars0 - Simple validator for SIRI 2.0 written in Python 3.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 5th - Feb 11th, 2018
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • GTFS-realtime VehiclePositions to GTFS-realtime TripUpdates (TransitClock) - Java application that can consume raw vehicle positions and generate prediction times in formats such as GTFS-realtime. Formerly known as "Transitime".

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 22nd - Jan 28th, 2018
item.description:
Software for Creating APIs

  • TransiCast - Provides public transportation data for North America in a single, integrated call and response format. The data is provided in stream-parsable XML and JSON formats. Open-source on Google Code. Hosted version at www.transitcast.com requires payment.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 15th - Jan 21st, 2018
item.description:
GTFS Realtime (and Other Real-time API) Archival Tools

  • retro-gtfsstars46 - A Python application that collects real-time data from the Nextbus API and archives it into the GTFS format (i.e., retrospective GTFS).

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 1st - Jan 7th, 2018
item.description:
Other multimodal data formats

  • General Bikeshare Feed Specification (GBFS)stars640 - Open data standard for real-time bikeshare information developed by members of the North American Bikeshare Association (NABSA).
    • gbfs-validatorstars10 - 3rd party tool to validate GBFS feeds.
    • gbfs R packagestars0 - Functions to interface with GBFS feeds in R, allowing users to save and accumulate tidy .rds datasets for specified cities/bikeshare programs.
    • GBFS Documentation Platform - Documentation platform for the General Bikeshare Feed Specification, maintaned by MobilityData. View the GBFS specification, change proposals, policy guides, and technical tools.
  • GTFS-plusstars19 - A GTFS-based transit network format for vehicle and capacity data suitable for dynamic transit modeling developed by Puget Sound Regional Council, UrbanLabs LLC, LMZ LLC, and San Francisco County Transportation Authority.
  • GTFS-statstars5 - An extension to a GTFS transit network with additional files that contain performance data developed by UrbanLabs LLC and San Francisco County Transportation Authority.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Nov 27th - Dec 3rd, 2017
item.description:
GTFS Converters

  • GTFS-OSM-Syncstars83 - A Java tool for synchronizing data in GTFS format with OpenStreetMap.org.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Oct 2nd - Oct 8th, 2017
item.description:
GTFS Validators

  • GTFS Data Package Specificationstars20 - A Data Package specification with validation accomplished with Good Tables. Includes a data package, schemas, tests, and uses South East Queensland GTFS data as an example.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Sep 18th - Sep 24th, 2017
item.description:
Python
  • partridgestars122 - A fast, forgiving Python GTFS reader built on pandas DataFrames.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Sep 4th - Sep 10th, 2017
item.description:
Getting started

  • GTFS - A GTFS feed is a group of text files that contains infrequently changing transit data, like stops, routes, trips, and other schedule data. Transit agencies typically update their GTFS feed every few months.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 21st - Aug 27th, 2017
item.description:
Getting started

  • GTFS Realtime - GTFS Realtime consists of three binary files that contain realtime vehicle positions, realtime arrival information, and service alerts. Transit agencies typically update these files every minute.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jul 10th - Jul 16th, 2017
item.description:
GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • GTFS.html - An entirely browser-based tool to view GTFS feeds. Use it to view routes, stops, timetables, etc.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 12th - Jun 18th, 2017
item.description:
Web Apps (open source)

  • Google I/O Transport Trackerstars547 - Shows shuttle arrival times for Google I/O conference, based on the open-source transport-tracker projectstars547. Note: To implement this yourself, you need a Google Maps APIs Premium Plan license.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Apr 3rd - Apr 9th, 2017
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Libraries & Demo Apps

  • gtfs-realtime-bindingsstars300 - The official bindings for Java, .NET, Node.js, Python, and Ruby generated from the official GTFS-realtime protocol buffer specification.

    • SIRI

  • SIRI REST Client - An open-source Android library for interacting with the RESTful SIRI interface for real-time transit data, such as that currently being used by the MTA Bus Time API.
  • SIRI 1.3 POJOs (Android-compatible) - Android-compatible Plain Old Java Objects (POJOSs) used for data binding (deserliazing XML/JSON) responses for SIRI v1.3 APIs. Used by the SIRI REST Client.
  • King County Metro Legacy AVL to SIRI - Java-based tool to convert King County Metro's Legacy AVL format to SIRI.

    • GTFS Realtime (and Other Real-time API) Archival Tools

  • gtfsrdbstars28 - A Python tool that supports reading and archiving GTFS-realtime feeds into a database

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 20th - Mar 26th, 2017
item.description:
GTFS

  • GTFS Best Practices - Best practices for producers of a GTFS feed.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 27th - Mar 5th, 2017
item.description:
Native Apps (closed source)

  • Transit Display - Multimodal and real-time transit display software.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 23rd - Jan 29th, 2017
item.description:
Native Apps (open source)

  • Transportrstars828 An Android app that uses public-transport-enablerstars324 in order to connect to many different transport networks worldwide.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 2nd - Jan 8th, 2017
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • NextBus API to GTFS-realtime - A Java-based command-line utility to convert from the NextBus API format to GTFS-realtime. Note that NextBus now directly offers a GTFS-realtime API for their products. See Cubic site and this FAQ.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Sep 19th - Sep 25th, 2016
item.description:
Native Apps (closed source)

  • Transit
    • JavaScript
  • gtfs-sequelizestars19 - Node.js library modeling the static GTFS using sequelize.js.
    • Python
  • gtfslib-pythonstars41 - An open source library in python for reading GTFS files and computing various stats and indicators about Public Transport networks.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jun 6th - Jun 12th, 2016
item.description:
Web Apps (open source)

  • Transitive.jsstars653 - Creates a customizable web map layer of transit routes using Leaflet or D3.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit May 16th - May 22nd, 2016
item.description:
Community

  • Transit Developers mailing list
  • OneBusAway
    • OneBusAway User mailing list
    • OneBusAway Developers mailing list
    • OneBusAway API mailing list
    • OneBusAway Slack chat
  • TransitWiki - A community wiki for transit planners. Like this repo, but better.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Mar 14th - Mar 20th, 2016
item.description:
Hardware

  • Bus Tracking GPSstars25 - Code for Miami prototype of a cheap open-source solution to track transit buses.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Feb 15th - Feb 21st, 2016
item.description:
Software for Creating APIs

  • OpenTripPlanner - An open source platform for multi-modal and multi-agency journey planning, as well as returning information about a multi-modal graph (using data sources such as GTFS and OpenStreetMap).

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Jan 18th - Jan 24th, 2016
item.description:
Native Apps (closed source)

  • ally
  • CityMapper

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Sep 14th - Sep 20th, 2015
item.description:
Agency Tools

  • Remix - A webapp that lets transit agencies easily plan routes.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 31st - Sep 6th, 2015
item.description:
GTFS Data Collection and Maintenance Tools

  • bus-routerstars33 - Python script that generates missing shapes.txt for GTFS using routing from Google Maps Directions API or OSRM.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 24th - Aug 30th, 2015
item.description:
Native Apps (closed source)

  • TransLoc Rider - Real-time transit maps for over 100 transit systems.

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 17th - Aug 23rd, 2015
item.description:
Native Apps (open source)

  • OpenTripPlanner Android - An Android app for OpenTripPlanner
  • OpenTripPlanner iOSstars66 - An iOS app for OpenTripPlanner

    • Native Apps (closed source)

  • Moovit

item.title: CUTR-at-USF/awesome-transit Aug 10th - Aug 16th, 2015
item.description:
GTFS Realtime Convertors

  • Syncromatics API to GTFS-realtimestars2 - A Java-based command-line utility to convert from the Syncromatics API format to GTFS-realtime TripUpdates and VehiclePositons.
  • KV6,15,17, and ARNU to GTFS-realtimestars1 - Java-based tool to process incoming KV6,15,17 and ARNU and match them to static transit data present in a RID integration database. It then proceeds to export this data as ARNU RITinfo, GTFS(realtime) and KV78turbo
  • Civic Transitstars3 - Screen-scrapes KCATA’s TransitMaster WebWatch installation to produce a GTFS-realtime feed.
  • WMATA BusPositions API to GTFS-realtimestars11 - Java-based tool to convert from WMATA's BusPositions API and Alert RSS feeds from MetroAlerts to GTFS-realtime TripUpdates, VehiclePositions, and Alerts feeds.
  • SEPTA API to GTFS-realtimestars2 - Java-based tool to convert SEPTA's real-time bus and rail data to GTFS-realtime
  • CTA API to GTFS-realtimestars5 - Java-based tool to convert CTA's Train Tracker data to GTFS-realtime.
  • Detroit DOT to GTFS-realtimestars2 - Extract real-time info from DDOT's TransitMaster installation (database) and convert to GTFS-realtime
  • Live Transit Event Triggerstars4 - Extracts data from Ride On's OrbCAD database and export as GTFS-realtime.
  • SoundTransit to GTFS-realtimestars3 - Convert text file feed from Sound Transit to GTFS-realtime

    • Web Apps (open source)

  • Transit-Mapstars325 - Web app that animates vehicles (markers) on a map using the public transport timetables to interpolate their positions along the routes (polylines).
  • OpenTripPlanner Client GWTstars7 - A Google Web Toolkit-based web interface for OpenTripPlanner
  • GTFS-realtime Alerts Producer Web Applicationstars1 - A Java-based web application for producing GTFS-realtime Service Alerts.
  • HRT BUS Web appstars18 - HRT Bus API publishes real time bus data from Hampton Roads Transit through an application programming interface for developers to make apps from it.
  • Instabus - Realtime map of Austin's (CapMetro) public transit. Has no server/backend dependency at all and runs completely on GitHub pages.
    • Java
  • OneBusAway GTFS Modules - A Java-based library for reading, writing, and transforming public transit data in the GTFS format, including database support.

    • GTFS Converters

  • Chouette - Converts French-Transmodel, SIRI, NETeX. See Chouette.mobi website for more info.

    • GTFS Realtime Libraries & Demo Apps

  • GTFS-realtime Exporter - A Java-based tool that assists in producing and sharing a GTFS-relatime feed.
  • GTFS-realtime Alerts Producer Demo - A Java-based demo project for producing GTFS-realtime Service Alerts.
  • GTFS-realtime Alerts Producer Web Applicationstars1 - A Java-based web application for producing GTFS-realtime Service Alerts.
  • GTFS-realtime TripUpdates & VehiclePositions Producer Demo - A Java-based demo project for producing GTFS-realtime TripUpdates (estimated arrivals) and Vehicle Positions.

    • Software for Creating APIs

  • OneBusAway - A Java app that consumes GTFS and GTFS-Realtime (along with other formats) and turns them into an easy to use REST API.
